description: Arthur C. Clarke's classic science fiction novel about human evolution, artificial intelligence, and first contact with a vastly superior intelligence. From the dawn of man to the star-child, the novel traces humanity's transformation through encounters with the mysterious monolith.
useCase: Explore one of the most influential science fiction works ever written — the novel that asked: what happens when humanity meets its makers, and what will we become?
knownRisks: The novel was written alongside the film adaptation; differences exist between the book and Kubrick's film.
Some scientific and technological concepts (HAL 9000, space travel) reflect 1968-era understanding.
The novel deals with themes of evolution and cosmic intelligence that may be considered speculative.
